Finding.description: One piece of evidence, grounded in the rendered input facts.
Finding.title: Finding

json_schema.description: Shared response shape for the source investigators.
json_schema.title: InvestigatorResponse

messages.content:
INCIDENT WINDOW
start: 2026-06-12T08:44:00+00:00
end: ongoing
rule: alert.triggered_at (2026-06-12T09:14:00+00:00) minus 30m lookback; deviated series not recovered -> ongoing

ALERT
id: alert_cm_001
title: Checkout error rate above 1% on checkout-service
service: checkout-service
triggered_at: 2026-06-12T09:14:00+00:00
monitoring severity label: major
signal: error_rate_pct
threshold: 1.0
observed value at trigger: 3.6
description: error rate sustained above 1% for 10 minutes

DEPLOYS AND CHANGES: none - the change window was checked and no deploys, config changes, or flag flips were found (this is evidence)

TOPOLOGY (service -> depends on)
- checkout-service (service) -> payment-gateway, session-cache
- payment-gateway (third_party) -> (nothing)
- session-cache (cache) -> (nothing)

request.system:
You are one specialist in an evidence-first incident investigation pipeline
for a SaaS engineering team. You receive validated facts extracted from an
offline incident package - never raw files, never live systems.

Non-negotiable rules:
- Cite only information present in the input. Never invent timestamps,
  services, signals, or values.
- Copy timestamps exactly as they appear in the input (ISO-8601).
- Every finding's interpretation states what the data shows and how strongly;
  say "consistent with" rather than "proves" when evidence is indirect.
- You investigate and describe. You never recommend executing actions.
- If the input is insufficient to answer something in your remit, record it
  in `gaps` instead of guessing.
- Findings must be specific enough that another engineer could verify each
  one against the same input in seconds.

Role: deploy correlation investigator.
Input: deploys/config/flag changes, the alert, the incident window, and the
service topology.

Report as findings with exact timestamps:
- for each change: how long before/after symptom onset it landed, and
  whether that gap is consistent with causing the incident
- whether the changed service can reach the affected service through the
  topology (name the path), or cannot
- changes that are poor candidates (wrong service, wrong time, no path) -
  ruling out is evidence
Timing correlation is not causation: state alignment strength, not verdicts.

response.text: {"findings":[{"interpretation":"deploys.json is an empty list: the change window was checked and nothing shipped - internal change ruled out as trigger","timestamp":null,"service":"checkout-service","signal":null,"value":null}],"gaps":[],"reasoning":"compared data against baselines"}
